response.justification: While Bogdan brings genuine geospatial experience (GISCollective, Green Map GIS app, FOSS4G talk) and demonstrated high-throughput systems work at Sociomantic, the role's core requirement — production-grade C++ or Rust — is critically unmet. His only C++ work was a 6-month stint in 2014–2015 writing components for a C IDE, and no Rust experience appears anywhere on the resume. His career trajectory has moved firmly toward TypeScript, Node.js, and full-stack web development, with his current Staff Engineer role at Stripe confirming that direction. The GIS domain overlap is encouraging but insufficient to bridge the language and pipeline-depth gap this role demands.
strengths: Real geospatial platform development experience with deployed systems used by NGOs and conservation professionals
Proven high-performance, low-latency systems work at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O and fibers
Broad Linux and distributed-systems exposure across embedded, mobile, and server-side contexts over a 15+ year career
concerns: No commercial C++ since 2015 and zero Rust experience — the primary technical requirement for this role is unmet
Current and recent trajectory (Stripe, Shopify, RIDE) is firmly TypeScript/Node.js; no signal of a return to systems-level programming
GIS experience is at the data-collection and visualization layer, not at the routing pipeline, tiling, or navigation-accuracy optimization layer this role targets
